findings.finding: Task 5's marker regex enforces 30-char minimum, numeric lifecycle-id, and YYYY-MM-DD format, but no regression fixture probes boundary conditions (29-char rationale, alpha lifecycle-id, malformed date).
Task 6's grep -c verification can be satisfied by any two mentions of test-skill-design regardless of whether the recipe body actually references the four new test files.
findings.evidence_quote: grep -c "test-skill-design" justfile ≥ 2 AND just test-skill-design exits 0.

findings.finding: Test #3's per-form coverage gate >= 1 is trivially satisfied and gives no signal about whether the audit's specific named drift mode (lifecycle plan.md → skills/critical-review/SKILL.md citation drift) is actually exercised.

Task 4 declares Depends on: none but the Context block requires reading tests/test_lifecycle_references_resolve.py to understand FORM_REGEXES structure before editing — reading-comprehension prerequisite not edge-listed.
findings.evidence_quote: Depends on: none (does NOT depend on Task 1 helpers) ... Read the file before editing to confirm.

findings.finding: Task 6 insertion point at 'around line 435' has no deterministic resolution procedure when the line drifts.
findings.evidence_quote: around line 435 — this line drifts; the plan acknowledges 're-check before editing'

findings.finding: Plugin enumeration includes the cortex-core mirror as a byte-identical file copy. Path.resolve() doesn't deduplicate it, so Test #4 produces dual failure messages on any cap breach.
findings.evidence_quote: Skill enumeration globs plugins/*/skills/*/SKILL.md which includes the cortex-core plugin mirror

findings.finding: Task 3 (handoff schema test) has not earned its place: zero historical handoff-rename incidents are cited and empirical pre-audit shows all current pairs satisfied.
